message.content:
You are a SEALED implementer under the barrier protocol at /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md — read it first and comply strictly.
ACCESS RULES: You must NOT read any file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/, and NOT read any Haskell source (*.hs) of tamarin-prover anywhere on disk (notably /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/{lib,src}/ and /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/{lib,src}/). Do NOT read /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/INTEGRATION_REPORT.md. No web access. You MAY: run the Haskell reference via /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/oracle/wf_oracle.sh (it runs the HS binary on a .spthy and prints its output, wf block included — no --prove needed; pass extra flags after the file), read .spthy example files anywhere under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/examples/, and read/write freely inside /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/. No git commits.
DISCIPLINE: log every oracle interaction in the workspace QUERIES.log; record behavioral facts in BEHAVIOR.md with provenance; append a round-5 section to a REPORT file (fold into BEHAVIOR.md if .md creation is blocked). Code comments describe current behavior only. Every behavioral claim traces to a logged oracle probe or a captured target. Return a PLAIN-TEXT summary.

Crate: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/workspace/wf-clean (self-contained, own AST; the wf checker logic lives in src/{checks.rs,formula.rs,report.rs}).

THE TASK — your wf checker diverges from the reference on 71 real corpus theories. The exact list is /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/round5/wf_gate_diffs.txt; each theory's CORRECT reference wf-block is pre-materialized (HS oracle output) at round5/targets/<path-with-slashes-as-underscores>.hs.txt; round5/families.tsv classifies each into one of three families. Make your checker reproduce the reference wf-block for all 71 (and keep every currently-passing theory passing). Probe the oracle freely to characterize correct behavior; the targets are your ground truth.

FAMILY 1 — guardedness OVER-report (49 files). Your checker emits a " Formula guardedness" block ("Lemma `X' cannot be converted to a guarded formula: unguarded variable(s) …") for lemmas the reference accepts silently (the target blocks have NO guardedness section — they are mostly accountability protocols whose `accounts for` lemmas are translated into generated verification lemmas with primed variables like Hp/Sp/sid). Your guardedness DECISION is incomplete: it rejects formula shapes the reference converts successfully. Probe the reference's guardedness acceptance exhaustively — feed it constructed lemmas spanning the shapes in the 49 targets (nested quantifiers, disjunction/implication/negation structure, action-atom guards reachable through various connectives, quantified variables guarded transitively, the translated-accountability shapes) and pin exactly which formulas it accepts as guarded. Rework your guardedness conversion/decision so it accepts everything the reference accepts (and still reports the genuinely-unguarded cases that your existing passing fixtures cover). This is the hard core of the round.

FAMILY 2 — sort/capitalization OVER-report (15 files). Your checker emits "Variable with mismatching sorts or capitalization" where the reference does not (and in some cases duplicates the "Possible reasons" preamble). Probe which variable spellings the reference actually flags as sort/capitalization clashes vs which it accepts; fix the detection to match. Watch for the case where the same variable appears with a suffix-sort spelling in one place and a sigil spelling in another — the reference may treat these as the same variable.

FAMILY 3 — tail (7 files, families.tsv "tail"). Distinct smaller divergences to pin against their targets: (a) a "Lemma annotations" topic the reference emits that you omit; (b) the multi-entry "Facts occur in the left-hand-side but not in any right-hand-side" numbered list — the reference indents each numbered item with THREE leading spaces and separates consecutive entries with a "  " blank line, your rendering uses two spaces / no separators; (c) SAPIC/accountability-deprecated theories where the reference reports MORE checks than you (you under-report). Pin each against its target and fix.

Keep every existing wf-clean test green; add tests/fixtures pinning representative members of each family. Do NOT edit anything outside your workspace.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
43	   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
44	   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
45	4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
46	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
47	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
48	   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
49	   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.
50	
51	## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)
52	
53	Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
54	diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
55	sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
56	every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
57	`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).
58	
59	- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
60	  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
61	  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
62	- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
63	  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
64	- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
65	  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.
66	
67	Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
68	reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
69	code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
70	replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
71	code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.
72

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# OOM guard: prover is the preferred kill target, capped at 24GB address space
echo 1000 > /proc/self/oom_score_adj 2>/dev/null || true
ulimit -v 25165824 2>/dev/null || true
# Black-box oracle: prints the HS tamarin-prover's full output (incl. the
# wellformedness WARNING section). Usage: wf_oracle.sh <file.spthy> [extra args e.g. --diff]
export PATH="/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:$PATH"
f="$1"; shift
exec timeout ${ORACLE_TIMEOUT:-60} "/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/.stack-work/install/x86_64-linux-tinfo6/ec0cb11b1bfcf8776d45e0357bbc6d6ff2077f9222735af22115429c8cdfcef1/9.6.7/bin/tamarin-prover" "$f" "$@" 2>&1
captures
examples
capture_all.sh
wf_oracle.sh

1	# Behavioral spec inferred from the oracle (wellformedness checker)
2	
3	All facts below are derived from oracle observation (probes in
4	scratchpad/probes, captures in oracle/captures). Nothing here is taken from
5	tamarin-prover source.
6	
7	## Overall report shape
8	
9	The oracle emits the wellformedness result inside the pretty-printed theory,
10	wrapped in a block comment.
11	
12	Success (empty report):
13	
14	    /* All wellformedness checks were successful. */
15	
16	Failure (>=1 topic):
17	
18	    /*
19	    WARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!
20	
21	    <block1>
22	
23	    <block2>
24	    ...
25	    */
26	
27	Inner text (what `render_report` produces for a non-empty report) is:
28	
29	    "WARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!" + "\n\n" + join(blocks, "\n\n")
30	
31	Each `block` = topic header + "\n" + underline + "\n\n" + body, where
32	`underline` = '=' repeated char_count(topic) (topic length INCLUDING any
33	leading/trailing space). Bodies may themselves contain blank lines.
34	
35	`render_report` for the empty report returns the success line
36	`/* All wellformedness checks were successful. */`.
37	`underline_topic(t)` returns `t + "\n" + "=".repeat(char_count(t))`.
38	
39	## Canonical topic order (report order)
40	
41	Observed by kitchen-sink probes ks1 / ks3 and the round-2 ordering probes
42	(comb1, ord_tail, mega_tail, diff_mega, diff_place):
43	
44	 1. `Unbound variables`
45	 2. `Fresh public constants`                             (round2; always-on)
46	 3. `Public constants with mismatching capitalization`   (public-names report)
47	 4. `Variable with mismatching sorts or capitalization`
48	 5. `Reserved names`
49	 5a. `Reserved prefixes`                                 (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
50	 6. `Fr facts must only use a fresh- or a msg-variable`
51	 7. `Special facts`
52	 7b. `Fact capitalization issues`                        (round3; before arity)
53	 8. `Fact arity issues`                                  (non-diff; diff merges these
54	 9. `Fact multiplicity issues`                            two into "Fact usage" - OUT OF SCOPE)
55	10. `Facts occur in the left-hand-side but not in any right-hand-side ` (TRAILING space)
56	10a. `Left rule` / `Right rule`                          (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
57	10b. `Quantifier sorts`                                  (round4; per-item bundle)
58	11. `Formula terms`
59	12. ` Formula guardedness`   (LEADING space)
60	    NOTE (round4): topics 10b/11/12 are NOT globally fixed - they are emitted
61	    per formula item (see "Formula-check bundle" below); their relative order
62	    depends on which item emits which topic first.
63	13. `Lemma annotations`                                  (round2; always-on)
64	14. `Multiplication restriction of rules`                (round2; always-on)
65	15. `Nat Sorts`
66	16. `Subterm Convergence Warning`
67	17. `Message Derivation Checks` (appended by binary; needs Maude - OUT OF SCOPE)
68	    / `Derivation Checks` (timeout variant - OUT OF SCOPE)
69	
70	Round-2 ordering evidence: comb1 -> `Unbound, Fresh public constants,
71	Public names, mismatching sorts, Reserved`; mega_tail -> `Formula terms,
72	Formula guardedness, Lemma annotations, Multiplication restriction, Nat Sorts,
73	Subterm`; diff_mega/diff_place -> `... Reserved names, Reserved prefixes,
74	Fr facts, ... lhs-not-rhs, Left/Right rule, Formula terms, ...`.
75	
76	Public names (#3) is generated separately and *inserted before* the first of a
77	set of anchor topics = every topic from #4 (mismatching sorts) onward.
78	`after_public_names_topics()` returns that anchor list; `insert_wf_before(
79	report, errs, anchors)` inserts `errs` immediately before the first report
80	entry whose topic is in `anchors` (appends at end if none present). Unbound
81	variables (#1) and Fresh public constants (#2) are emitted before the
82	public-names insertion point (so they are NOT anchors).
83	
84	### Diff-mode-only vs. always-on
85	
86	Gated on `theory.is_diff`: `Reserved prefixes`, `Left rule`, `Right rule`
87	(observed silent in non-diff mode; diff_reserved_prefix without `--diff` emits
88	nothing). Always-on (both modes): `Fresh public constants`, `Lemma
89	annotations`, `Multiplication restriction of rules`.
90	
91	## Per-check triggers and message templates
92	
93	### Unbound variables
94	Trigger: a protocol rule uses a variable in actions/conclusions not bound by a
95	premise. Entries joined by "\n  \n":
96	    "  rule `NAME' has unbound variables: \n    v1, v2"
97	(trailing space after "variables:"). Vars sorted, ", " joined, term-printed.
98	
99	### Public constants with mismatching capitalization (public_names_report)
100	Trigger: two public constants whose names are equal under ASCII-lowercasing but
101	differ in capitalization. Body:
102	    "Identifiers are case-sensitive, mismatched capitalizations are considered as different, i.e., 'ID' is different from 'id'. Check the capitalization of your identifiers.\n\n" + numbered items
103	Each lowercased-key group -> one item "  N. " + occurrences. Occurrences group by
104	location; same loc: `rule "R1":  name 'Alice', 'alice'`; different locs joined
105	", ": `rule "R1":  name 'Server', rule "R2":  name 'server'`. Names ASCII-sorted.
106	`public_names_report_from_pairs` takes raw (location, name) pairs.
107	
108	### Variable with mismatching sorts or capitalization
109	Trigger: within one rule, two variables share a base name but differ in sort
110	prefix ($x/x/~x) or capitalization. Fixed header:
111	    "Possible reasons:\n1. Identifiers are case sensitive, i.e.,'x' and 'X' are considered to be different.\n2. The same holds for sorts:, i.e., '$x', 'x', and '~x' are considered to be different.\n"
112	per rule: "  rule `A5': \n    1. $s, ~s" (trailing space; numbered variant groups).
113	
114	### Reserved names
115	Trigger: rule uses a fact with a reserved name in a position. The reserved set
116	is POSITION-DEPENDENT (observed z9/z11/z12):
117	  - left-hand-side / right-hand-side: {K, KU, KD}
118	  - the middle (actions): {K, KU, KD, In, Out, Fr}  (the I/O facts are also
119	    reserved as actions; on LHS/RHS they are "Special facts" instead)
120	Per (rule, position) entry joined "\n  \n":
121	    "  Rule `R1' contains facts with reserved names on left-hand-side:\n    <facts>"
122	Position phrases: LHS `on left-hand-side:`, actions `on the middle:`,
123	RHS `on the right-hand-side:`. Facts via fact printer, fsep comma-joined.
124	
125	### Fr facts must only use a fresh- or a msg-variable
126	Trigger: `Fr(t)` where t is not fresh/msg var. Entries joined "\n  \n":
127	    "  rule `R1' fact: Fr( $p )"
128	
129	### Special facts
130	Trigger: reserved I/O fact in disallowed position (Out/Fr/K in premises,
131	In/K in conclusions). Per (rule, side) joined "\n  \n":
132	    "  rule `R1' uses disallowed facts on left-hand-side:\n    Out( x )"
133	(sides `on left-hand-side:`/`on right-hand-side:`; lowercase "rule").
134	
135	### Fact arity issues
136	Trigger: same fact name with >1 arity. Body:
137	    "Same fact is used with different arities, i.e., Fact('A','B') is different from Fact('A'). \nCheck the arguments of your facts.\n  " + factBlocks
138	facts sorted by lowercased name; factBlock (each prefixed "\n"):
139	    "\n  Fact `foo':\n\n" + items + "\n  "
140	item i: "    {i}. {Label} `{owner}', arity {n}\n         {render}"; items joined
141	"\n    \n". Rules and LEMMAS both contribute (interleaved in theory-item order);
142	restrictions do NOT (r3_restrarity). Rule facts: Label=`Rule`, render=fact pp.
143	Lemma action facts: Label=`Lemma`, render = raw Haskell `Fact {factTag =
144	ProtoFact {Linear|Persistent} "{name}" {arity}, factAnnotations = fromList [],
145	factTerms = [{de-Bruijn terms}]}` (r3_lemarity). Rule items dedup per
146	(label,owner,arity); lemma facts likewise. Multiplicity is analogous and ALSO
147	gathers lemma facts (r3_lemmult, item = `... multiplicity (persistence) ...`).
148	
149	### Fact multiplicity issues
150	Same structure keyed on multiplicity; header:
151	    "Same fact is used with different multiplicities, i.e., !Fact() (Persistent fact) exists along with Fact() (Linear) in your rules. \nCheck the multiplicity (persistence) of your facts.\n  " + factBlocks
152	item i: "    {i}. {Label} `{owner}', multiplicity (persistence) {Linear|Persistent}\n         {render}".
153	
154	### Fact capitalization issues (round3; precedes Fact arity issues)
155	Trigger: two facts whose names are equal under ASCII-lowercasing but differ in
156	exact spelling (e.g. `Send` vs `SEND`; facts must start upper-case, so the
157	difference is in non-first letters). Same block shape as Fact arity, header:
158	    "Fact names are case-sensitive, different capitalizations are considered as different facts, i.e., Fact() is different from FAct(). \nCheck the capitalization of your fact names.\n  " + factBlocks
159	Unlike arity, EVERY occurrence is listed (NO per-(rule,cap) dedup - `Send` twice
160	in one rule -> two items; r3_capord). item i:
161	    "    {i}. Rule `{rule}', capitalization \"{exactName}\"\n         {factPP}".
162	Groups keyed by lowercased name, sorted; a group needs >=2 distinct spellings.
163	Gathers rule facts across premises/actions/conclusions (source order).
164	
165	### Facts occur in the left-hand-side but not in any right-hand-side  (fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs)
166	Trigger: user fact identity (name,arity,mult) in some premise, never in any
167	conclusion. Numbered items joined "\n  \n":
168	    "  1. in rule \"r1\":  factName `X' arity: 0 multiplicity: Linear" +
169	      optional ". Perhaps you want to use the fact in rule \"r1\":  factName `Y' arity: 0 multiplicity: Linear"
170	suggestion = nearest RHS fact by edit distance under a threshold (omitted when
171	nothing close). Special facts excluded from both sides.
172	
173	### Fresh public constants (round2; always-on)
174	Trigger: a fresh-name literal (`~'foo'`, AST `Term::FreshLit`) used directly in
175	a rule. Constants collected in the order premises, conclusions, actions
176	(probe fpc_positions). Per rule with >=1 hit, entries joined "\n  \n":
177	    "  rule `NAME': fresh public constants are not allowed: ~'foo', ~'bar'"
178	constants rendered by the term printer. The list is a fillSep at width 69 with a
179	4-space continuation indent, begun after the header `  rule `{name}': fresh
180	public constants are not allowed:` (round3: r3_freshwrap; identical mechanism to
181	the Formula-terms list). CLOSED - was a documented gap.
182	
183	### Reserved prefixes (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
184	Trigger (diff theories only): a fact whose name starts with `DiffIntr` or
185	`DiffProto`. Facts collected in the order premises, actions, conclusions
186	(probe rp_multi). Per rule the body is:
187	    "  The Rule `NAME' contains facts with reserved prefixes ('DiffIntr',\n  'DiffProto') inside names:\n  \n" + factblocks
188	The HEADER is word-filled (Wadler `fillSep`) at WIDTH 69, indent 2 (measured via
189	rp_long/rp_med/rp_w47: a line breaks before the next word once column would
190	exceed 69). Words treat the rule name `\`NAME'` and each of `('DiffIntr',` /
191	`'DiffProto')` as single tokens. Each factblock (joined "\n  \n"):
192	    "    <fact_pp>\n    (ProtoFact <Mult> \"<name>\" <arity>,<arity>,<Mult>)"
193	The 2nd line is a raw Haskell Show of a (FactTag, arity, Multiplicity) tuple;
194	<Mult> is `Linear`/`Persistent`. GAP: multi-RULE joining unprobed (fixtures use
195	one rule).
196	
197	### Left rule / Right rule (round2; DIFF MODE ONLY)
198	Trigger (diff theories only): a diff rule (`Rule.left_right = Some((l,r))`)
199	whose explicit `left`/`right` projection differs from the parent rule projected
200	to that side (`diff(a,b)` -> a on left, -> b on right). Per rule the LEFT
201	projection is tested first; if inconsistent the RIGHT is not reported for that
202	rule (probe diff_both: both sides bad -> only "Left rule"). Body per entry
203	(joined "\n  \n"):
204	    "  Inconsistent left rule\n" + indent4(explicit_rule_pp) +
205	    "\n  \n  w.r.t.\n  \n" + indent4(parent_rule_pp)
206	("left"/"right" and topic "Left rule"/"Right rule"). Consistency compared via
207	the projected vs. explicit fact lists. Left rule topic precedes Right rule.
208	
209	### Lemma annotations (round2; always-on)
210	Trigger: a lemma with attribute `reuse` AND quantifier `exists-trace` (reuse on
211	`all-traces` is fine - probe la_alltraces). Entries joined "\n  \n":
212	    "  Lemma `NAME': cannot reuse 'exists-trace' lemmas"
213	(Topic may host other annotation conflicts not yet observed.)
214	
215	### Multiplication restriction of rules (round2; always-on)
216	Trigger: a rule whose CONCLUSIONS contain a multiplication (`*`, AST
217	`BinOp::Mult`) term. `*` only in premises or only in actions does NOT trigger
218	(probes mul_multi, mul_act); a `*` in a premise is not listed even when the
219	conclusion also has one (mul_pc). Per rule, entry (joined "\n  \n"):
220	    "  The following rule is not multiplication restricted:\n" + indent4(rule_pp) +
221	    "\n  \n  After replacing reducible function symbols in lhs with variables:\n" + indent4(rule2_pp) +
222	    "\n  \n    Terms with multiplication:  <terms>"
223	<terms> = maximal `*`-subterms of the conclusions, ", " joined (mul_terms).
224	GAPS: (a) rule2 (LHS reducible symbols replaced by fresh vars) is rendered equal
225	to rule1 - correct only when the LHS has no reducible symbols (probe mul_exp
226	shows `g^c` -> `x.1` otherwise); (b) the alternate failure mode
227	"Variables that occur only in rhs:  <vars>" (mul_exp) is not implemented; (c)
228	the co-emitted `Message Derivation Checks` block is Maude-derived, OUT OF SCOPE;
229	(d) rule_pp wrapping for long rules (see printer note).
230	
231	### Quantifier sorts  (round4)
232	Trigger: a lemma/restriction formula quantifies over a variable whose sort is
233	PUB or FRESH (message/temporal/nat quantifiers are allowed - probes
234	qs_pub_prefix/qs_fresh_prefix/qs_nat_prefix/qs_msg_plain). Per item:
235	    "  {Entity} `{name}' uses quantifiers with wrong sort: {tokens}"
236	Entity = `Lemma`/`Restriction` (both, probe qs_restr). Each offending variable
237	is a raw Haskell tuple token `("{name}",LSort{Pub|Fresh})`, collected in binding
238	order (outer binders before inner), rendered as a fillSep at width 69 with a
239	4-space continuation indent (same mechanism as Formula terms; probe qs_multi).
240	Multiple wrong-sort items join as separate emissions in the bundle (consecutive
241	ones merge with `\n  \n`; probe qs_twolem). This topic is emitted BEFORE Formula
242	terms within a single item (probe syn_pubsuffix).
243	
244	### Formula-variable sorts and sort-aware binding  (round4 - supersedes round2)
245	Formula quantifiers bind SORTED variables. A bound variable and a use are the
246	same variable only when they share a name AND a sort CLASS, so a temporal binder
247	`#x` does NOT bind a message-position use `x` (probe g1_core: `Free x`); a
248	message binder `x` does not bind a node use `#x` (g1_msgnode: `Free #x`). Sort
249	classes: {Msg, Untagged, Suffix(Msg)} collapse to Msg (this keeps a quantified
250	message var bound to its uses across the parser's Untagged-vs-Msg tagging - the
251	round2 false-positive fix, now done sort-aware instead of name-only);
252	Pub/Fresh/Node/Nat (and their Suffix forms) are each their own class. The de
253	Bruijn stack counts EVERY quantified var (temporals and sort-mismatched ones
254	included); the index is the distance to the innermost binder matching (name,
255	class) - probe g1_dbsort: `All x #x. ... h(x)` -> `h(Bound 1)` (msg x sits under
256	node #x). Same-name/different-sort binders coexist without capture (g1_samename:
257	`Ex x #x. A(x)@#x` -> SUCCESS).
258	
259	### Formula terms  (round3: FULL coverage; round4: sort-aware)
260	Trigger: a lemma/restriction formula uses a TERM "of the wrong form". The unit
261	checked is each ARGUMENT TERM of each atom (Eq/Less/.. -> both sides; Action ->
262	the TEMPORAL first, then the fact args; Pred/Last -> args). A term is wrong iff
263	it contains a FREE variable OR a REDUCIBLE function symbol; the WHOLE top-level
264	term is then reported (not the offending subterm). Body:
265	    "  {Entity} `{name}' uses terms of the wrong form: {termlist}\n  \n" + fixed_help
266	Entity = `Lemma` or `Restriction`. Terms are collected in source order and are
267	NOT deduplicated (`x=y & x=y` -> `Free y', `Free y'`). Reducibility is decided by
268	the CALLER, which supplies a set of reducible function-symbol names; the checker
269	only consumes it (entry point `formula_terms_reducible(thy, &reducible)`;
270	`formula_terms(thy)` = the empty-set convenience wrapper - free variables only).
271	
272	Raw term rendering (each term wrapped in `` `...' ``):
273	  - bound variable   -> `Bound N`   (de Bruijn: 0 = innermost binder; EVERY
274	                                      quantified var, incl. temporals, pushes one)
275	  - free variable    -> `Free <pp_var>`  (keeps the sort prefix, e.g. `Free #j`)
276	  - function app      -> `f(a,b)`   (args comma-joined, NO space)
277	  - tuple             -> `pair(a,pair(b,c))`  (right-nested binary pairs)
278	  - public constant   -> `'name'`
279	De Bruijn evidence: `All x y #i. h(x,y)` -> `h(Bound 2,Bound 1)`; nesting adds
280	depth (`All x #i.(Ex y #j. h(x))` -> `h(Bound 3)`). Free-inside-term evidence:
281	`f(y)` (f non-reducible, y free) -> `f(Free y)`; `f(h(x))` -> `f(h(Bound 1))`.
282	
283	Term-list layout is a fillSep at width 69 with a 4-space continuation indent,
284	begun after the header (`  {Entity} `{name}' uses terms of the wrong form:`): a
285	token (term + trailing comma, except the last) stays on the line while
286	`col + 1 + width <= 69`, else wraps. A long entity/name can push the entire list
287	to line 2; an over-wide single term also wraps to line 2. Multiple wrong lemmas:
288	each full block (header+list+help) is joined by `\n  \n`.
289	fixed_help = "  The only allowed terms are public constants and bound node and\n  message variables. If you encounter free message variables, then\n  you might have forgotten a #-prefix. Sort prefixes can only be\n  dropped where this is unambiguous. Moreover, reducible function\n  symbols are disallowed."
290	
291	### Formula guardedness (topic has LEADING space)  (round4: decision procedure)
292	Trigger: a LEMMA formula not convertible to guarded form. (An unguarded
293	RESTRICTION is a FATAL error, not a warning -- observed gr_restr/z1; lemmas
294	only.) Body:
295	    "  Lemma `{name}' cannot be converted to a guarded formula:\n    {reason}\n      \"<sub>\"\n    in the formula\n      \"<whole>\""
296	where <sub> is the FAILING QUANTIFIER SUBTREE and <whole> is the full lemma
297	formula. Exactly two reasons:
298	  - `unguarded variable(s) '<v1>', '<v2>' in the subformula` (vars in binding
299	    order, ", " joined, each `'<pp_var>'`; probes ge_unguard/qs_multi)
300	  - `universal quantifier without toplevel implication`
301	
302	DECISION PROCEDURE (pre-order; first failure reported, antecedent before
303	consequent, left before right - probes gr_both/gr_sib):
304	  - `∀ vars. body`: body MUST be a top-level implication `guard ==> rest`;
305	    otherwise "without toplevel implication" (bare atom, conjunction,
306	    disjunction, negation, iff ALL fail this way - gu_bareatom/gu_conj/gu_disj/
307	    gu_neg/gu_iff). If it is `guard ==> rest`, every quantified var must be in
308	    the guard set, else "unguarded variable(s)"; then recurse into guard then
309	    rest.
310	  - `∃ vars. body`: every quantified var must be in the guard set of `body`,
311	    else "unguarded variable(s)" (a non-conjunction body - disjunction/
312	    implication/negation - guards nothing: ge_disj/ge_impl/ge_neg); then recurse
313	    into body. A bare action atom or a conjunction of them is a valid guard
314	    (ge_bareact/ge_conj_ok).
315	  - GUARD SET of a formula = the (name, sort-class) keys of variables occurring
316	    in ACTION atoms reachable through CONJUNCTIONS only. Disjunction, negation,
317	    implication, equality/`<`/`⋖`/`⊏`/`last` atoms, and nested quantifiers
318	    contribute NO guards (gx_disj_ant/gx_neg_ant/gx_eq_ant/gx_less/gx_last/
319	    gx_quant_g). An action atom guards all its fact-arg vars AND its temporal
320	    (gx_pair). Guard matching is sort-aware (syn_pubsuffix: pub `$x` is NOT
321	    guarded by a msg `x` in `A(x)`). This is the only semantic change from the
322	    round-3 heuristic (which recursed guards through Or/Not/quantifiers).
323	  - `¬`, `∧`, `∨`, `⇒`, `⇔` at the top: recurse into operands (left first).
324	  - Any non-quantified formula: no failure (gt_noquant/gt_topconj).
325	
326	MULTI-LINE FORMULA PRINTER (pp_formula_wrapped, col-relative; round4 fix).
327	Page width 72 (single line fits at total col 72, breaks by 74). The formula is
328	embedded as `      "..."`, so its first char is at column 7 (`pp_formula_wrapped(
329	f, 7)`). Continuation lines break relative to the enclosing group's START column
330	`col`, not a fixed base (round3's base+hang was off by one at the top level -
331	gt_and_two_all):
332	  - Action/Last/Pred atom -> single-line text (never breaks).
333	  - relational atom `a OP b` (=,<,⋖,⊏): breaks AFTER the operator; the right
334	    term hangs at `col+0`; the term operands are NOT parenthesised (gnest).
335	  - logical binary op `(a) OP (b)`: breaks after OP; right operand hangs at
336	    `col+0` (the group's start column, i.e. just after any enclosing `(`).
337	  - `¬` -> `¬` beside a parenthesised operand (never breaks at the ¬).
338	  - quantifier `Q vars. body`: breaks after the `.`; body hangs at `col+1`
339	    (measured gnest: a nested quantifier at col 9 -> body at col 10).
340	When a formula fits on one line the printer is byte-identical to the single-line
341	`pp_formula`, so narrow-formula fixtures (p21/r3_guard_*) are unaffected.
342	GAP: the printer does NOT do capture-avoiding alpha-renaming; when a bound var
343	name collides with a free var of the same name the oracle renames the bound one
344	(`$x.1` in syn_pubsuffix). Fixtures avoid such collisions (guardedness fixtures
345	use distinct names, and the Quantifier-sorts fixtures use consistently-bound
346	prefix vars so no free collision arises).
347	
348	### Nat Sorts
349	Trigger: var used in %+ (nat) context not of sort nat. Entries joined "\n  \n":
350	    "  y in term (y%+z) must be of sort nat" (nat-sort inference - structural only, gap).
351	
352	### Subterm Convergence Warning
353	Trigger: user equation l=r not subterm convergent. Body:
354	    "  User-defined equations must be convergent and have the finite variant property. The following equations are not subterm convergent. If you are sure that the set of equations is nevertheless convergent and has the finite variant property, you can ignore this warning and continue \n\n" + eqs + "\n   \n For more information, please refer to the manual : https://tamarin-prover.com/manual/master/book/010_modeling-issues.html "
355	each eq "    {lhsPP} = {rhsPP}" (subterm decision + printer partial - gap).
356	
357	## Term pretty-printer (observed)
358	- Var: sortprefix + base + ("."+idx if idx>0). Fresh `~`, Pub `$`, Nat `%`,
359	  Node `#`, Msg/Untagged ``.
360	- Pair / App("pair",[a,b]): `<a, b, ...>` (right-nested pairs flattened).
361	- App(f,args): `f(a, b)` (nullary -> `f`).
362	- PubLit s: `'s'`.
363	- BinOp: Exp `a^b` (no outer parens); Mult `(a*b)`; Union `(a++b)`;
364	  Xor `(a(+)b)` U+2295 AC-sorted; NatPlus `(a%+b)`.
365	- NatOne -> `%1`.
366	- Fact: [`!` if persistent] + name + "(" + (" "+args.join(", ")+" )" if args else " )").
367	
368	## Rule pretty-printer (observed; round2)
369	Used by Left/Right rule and Multiplication restriction. Base form (single line
370	when short):
371	    "rule (modulo E) NAME:\n   [ prems ] <arrow> [ concls ]"
372	- 3-space indent before the fact-list line; fact lists `[ f1, f2 ]`, `[ ]` empty.
373	- <arrow> = `-->` (no actions) or `--[ acts ]->` (actions present).
374	- `(modulo E)` (the AST modulo field, default "E").
375	- When embedded in a warning the whole 2-line block is indented (4 for
376	  Left/Right and Multiplication, giving 4 and 4+3=7 spaces).
377	GAP: long rules wrap - the arrow drops to its own line indented 6 and the
378	conclusions to indent 7 (probe mul_terms). Byte-parity fixtures use short rules.
379	
380	## Formula-check bundle assembly (round4)
381	The three per-formula checks - Quantifier sorts, Formula terms, Formula
382	guardedness - are NOT independent global checks. They are run item-by-item:
383	  - PROCESSING ORDER: every LEMMA (source order) first, then every RESTRICTION
384	    (source order) - lemmas precede restrictions even when a restriction is
385	    source-first (probes qs_restr, ord_rl_ft2).
386	  - Per item the topics are emitted in the SUB-ORDER [Quantifier sorts, Formula
387	    terms, Formula guardedness] (guardedness for lemmas only) - probe
388	    syn_pubsuffix.
389	  - The emission sequence of (topic, entry) is then rendered by MERGING ONLY
390	    CONSECUTIVE same-topic entries under one header (separator `\n  \n`). A
391	    topic that recurs after an intervening different topic starts a FRESH block
392	    with its own underline (probe ord_qs_ft_qs: QS,FT,QS renders THREE blocks;
393	    qs_twolem: two adjacent QS merge into one).
394	  - The bundle occupies the report slot after diff Left/Right and before Lemma
395	    annotations; `Lemma annotations` is a SEPARATE global check AFTER the bundle,
396	    NOT part of the per-item sub-order (probe ord_la_ft2: FT of a later lemma
397	    precedes Lemma annotations of an earlier one). Implemented as
398	    `checks::formula_reports(thy, reducible)`.
399	
400	## Formula variable identity (round2 fix -> superseded by round4 sort classes)
401	The round2 fix bound formula variables by NAME only to remove a spurious
402	"Formula terms"/" Formula guardedness" report on round2/exists_trace_reuse. Name-
403	only OVER-binds: it wrongly binds a temporal `#x` to a message use `x`
404	(g1_core). Round4 replaces it with sort-CLASS matching (see "Formula-variable
405	sorts and sort-aware binding"): {Untagged, Msg} collapse to one class (fixing the
406	round2 case) while `#x`/`$x`/`~x`/`%x` are distinct classes (fixing g1_core).
407	exists_trace_reuse still reports only "Lemma annotations".
408	
409	## Round 3 (Unit C) - closed and residual
410	
411	CLOSED with byte-parity fixtures:
412	- Formula terms FULL coverage: reducible functions + de Bruijn `Bound N`, free
413	  vars inside terms, no-dedup source order, fillSep(69) list wrap, multi-lemma
414	  `\n  \n` separator (`formula_terms_reducible` / `check_theory_with_reducible`).
415	- Fact capitalization issues topic.
416	- Lemma-sourced fact arity/multiplicity (raw Haskell `Fact {..}` render).
417	- Fresh-const list wrapping; multi-group public-names separator.
418	- Guardedness: nested-subformula selection, the "universal quantifier without
419	  toplevel implication" mode, and the multi-line formula printer (implies-break
420	  and And-break byte-exact).
421	
422	## Round 4 (Unit C) - closed and residual
423	
424	CLOSED with byte-parity fixtures (the two round-3 gaps that blocked full
425	post-elaboration replacement):
426	- GAP 1 - sort-aware quantifier binding: the wrong-form-terms de Bruijn stack
427	  now matches (name, sort-class); temporal/pub/fresh/nat binders no longer bind
428	  message uses. NEW topic "Quantifier sorts" (pub/fresh quantifiers) implemented
429	  with the fillSep list, Lemma/Restriction entities, and per-item ordering.
430	- GAP 2 - semantic guardedness: guard extraction is now a decision procedure
431	  (action atoms through conjunctions only; sort-aware guard matching), replacing
432	  the round-3 over-approximation that recursed guards through Or/Not/quantifiers.
433	  Universal/existential sub-modes, recursion order, and lemma-only scope all
434	  byte-exact.
435	- Formula-check bundle assembly (lemmas-then-restrictions, per-item sub-order,
436	  consecutive-merge) and the col-relative multi-line printer (top-level binop
437	  break and relational-atom break fixed).
438	
439	## Out-of-scope / known gaps
440	- Message Derivation Checks & Derivation Checks: computed by Maude after
441	  translation; not derivable from the AST. Not produced by check_theory.
442	- AC operator argument ordering (xor/multiset) needs the term ordering; the raw
443	  Formula-terms show of BinOp/AlgApp operators is a best-effort name guess.
444	- RULE-PRINTER wide wrapping (RESIDUAL, item 4b): a wide rule wraps to a
445	  multi-line form - `[` / facts-fillSep / `]` for a broken fact list, `-->` on
446	  its own line (indent 6), header at 4 - and in the Multiplication context is
447	  co-printed with the out-of-scope Maude derivation reprint. The single-line rule
448	  printer stays byte-exact for short rules (all current fixtures). Structure
449	  recorded from r3_rulewrap; a dedicated rule-layout engine is future work.
450	- FORMULA-PRINTER capture-avoidance: the oracle renames a bound var that collides
451	  with a same-name free var (`$x.1`); the clean printer does not. Fixtures avoid
452	  collisions. Also: quantifier HEAD wrapping (very long variable lists) and
453	  internal fact-argument wrapping are unprobed (fixtures stay within widths).
454	- Predicate atoms in formulas: the oracle expands `predicates:` before the wf
455	  check (gx_pred_grd renders `Pr(x)` as its definition `x = x`), so Pred atoms
456	  are not expected to reach the checker; guard extraction treats them as
457	  non-guarding defensively.
458	- Nat-sort inference / subterm-convergence decision: structural scaffolding only.
459

1	//! wf-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover wellformedness
2	//! checker, derived purely from black-box oracle behavior. See
3	//! workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the inferred behavioral spec.
4	
5	pub mod ast;
6	pub mod checks;
7	pub mod formula;
8	pub mod pretty;
9	pub mod report;
10	
11	pub use ast::*;
12	pub use report::{
13	    insert_wf_before, render_report, topics, underline_topic, WfError, WfReport, SUCCESS_LINE,
14	};
15	
16	/// Topics that follow the public-names report in the canonical order; the
17	/// public-names report is inserted immediately before the first of these that
18	/// is present in the report. (Unbound variables and Fresh public constants
19	/// precede the insertion point and are therefore NOT anchors.)
20	pub fn after_public_names_topics() -> Vec<&'static str> {
21	    vec![
22	        checks::T_SORTS,
23	        checks::T_RESERVED,
24	        checks::T_RESERVED_PREFIX,
25	        checks::T_FR,
26	        checks::T_SPECIAL,
27	        checks::T_FACT_CAP,
28	        checks::T_ARITY,
29	        checks::T_MULT,
30	        checks::T_LHSRHS,
31	        checks::T_LEFT,
32	        checks::T_RIGHT,
33	        checks::T_QUANT_SORTS,
34	        checks::T_FORMULA_TERMS,
35	        checks::T_GUARD,
36	        checks::T_LEMMA_ANNOT,
37	        checks::T_MULRESTRICT,
38	        checks::T_NAT,
39	        checks::T_SUBTERM,
40	    ]
41	}
42	
43	/// Run every check in the oracle's report order. The "Formula terms" check runs
44	/// in free-variable-only mode; for reducible-function coverage use
45	/// [`check_theory_with_reducible`].
46	pub fn check_theory(thy: &Theory) -> WfReport {
47	    check_theory_with_reducible(thy, &std::collections::BTreeSet::new())
48	}
49	
50	/// Run every check in the oracle's report order, passing a caller-computed set
51	/// of reducible function-symbol names to the "Formula terms" check (theories
52	/// with `equations:` make some user functions reducible; the caller derives
53	/// that set - typically from the equation theory / Maude - and this pipeline
54	/// only consumes it).
55	pub fn check_theory_with_reducible(
56	    thy: &Theory,
57	    reducible: &std::collections::BTreeSet<String>,
58	) -> WfReport {
59	    let mut report: WfReport = Vec::new();
60	    // Base pipeline (public names inserted afterwards at its anchor point).
61	    report.extend(checks::unbound_variables(thy)); // 1
62	    report.extend(checks::fresh_public_constants(thy)); // 2
63	    report.extend(checks::mismatching_sorts(thy)); // 4
64	    report.extend(checks::reserved_names(thy)); // 5
65	    report.extend(checks::reserved_prefixes(thy)); // 6 (diff mode only)
66	    report.extend(checks::fr_facts(thy)); // 7
67	    report.extend(checks::special_facts(thy)); // 8
68	    report.extend(checks::fact_capitalization(thy)); // 8b (precedes arity)
69	    report.extend(checks::fact_arity(thy)); // 9
70	    report.extend(checks::fact_multiplicity(thy)); // 10
71	    report.extend(checks::fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs(thy)); // 11
72	    report.extend(checks::diff_left_right(thy)); // 12, 13 (diff mode only)
73	    // The Quantifier-sorts / Formula-terms / Formula-guardedness bundle is
74	    // emitted per formula item (lemmas then restrictions), interleaved by topic.
75	    report.extend(checks::formula_reports(thy, reducible)); // 14, 15, 16
76	    report.extend(checks::lemma_annotations(thy)); // 17
77	    report.extend(checks::multiplication_restriction(thy)); // 17
78	    report.extend(checks::nat_sorts(thy)); // 18
79	    report.extend(checks::subterm_convergence(thy)); // 19
80	
81	    let pn = checks::public_names_report(thy); // 3
82	    let anchors = after_public_names_topics();
83	    insert_wf_before(&mut report, pn, &anchors);
84	    report
85	}
86	
87	/// Secondary entry point: report any of `lemma_names` that are not declared in
88	/// the theory. NOTE: this entry point's exact render string could not be
89	/// observed through the file oracle; the set-difference logic is verified but
90	/// the message text is a documented gap (see BEHAVIOR.md).
91	pub fn check_if_lemmas_in_theory(lemma_names: &[String], thy: &Theory) -> WfReport {
92	    let present = checks::theory_lemma_names(thy);
93	    let mut out = Vec::new();
94	    for name in lemma_names {
95	        if !present.contains(name) {
96	            out.push(WfError::new(
97	                "Lemmas",
98	                format!("lemma `{}' referenced but not present in theory", name),
99	            ));
100	        }
101	    }
102	    out
103	}
104	
105	// Re-export the public secondary entry points named in required_api.md.
106	pub use checks::{
107	    fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs, formula_terms, formula_terms_reducible, public_names_report,
108	    public_names_report_from_pairs,
109	};
110

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Materialize the Haskell reference wf-block for each of the 71 target theories
# into round5/targets/<safe>.hs.txt, using the sanctioned HS oracle only.
# Run this from inside the sealed workspace; it touches only the HS binary and
# the corpus .spthy inputs (both permitted materials).
set -u
here=$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)
CORPUS="${CORPUS:-/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/examples}"
FLAGS_MAP="${FLAGS_MAP:-/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/scripts/file_flags.tsv}"
ORACLE="$here/../oracle/wf_oracle.sh"
mkdir -p "$here/targets"
wf_block() {
  awk '
    /^\/\* All wellformedness checks were successful\. \*\/$/ { print; next }
    /^WARNING: the following wellformedness checks failed!$/  { f=1 }
    f { print }
    f && /^\*\/$/ { f=0 }'
}
while read -r REL; do
  f="$CORPUS/$REL"
  [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "MISSING $REL" >&2; continue; }
  fl=$(awk -F'\t' -v r="$REL" '!/^#/ && $1==r {print $2}' "$FLAGS_MAP")
  fl=${fl//@cd/}
  safe=$(printf '%s' "$REL" | tr '/' '_')
  # wf_oracle.sh runs the HS binary (no --prove needed — wf prints at load).
  "$ORACLE" "$f" $fl 2>/dev/null | wf_block > "$here/targets/$safe.hs.txt"
done < "$here/wf_gate_diffs.txt"
echo "wrote $(ls "$here/targets" | wc -l) HS reference blocks to targets/"
# Round 4 report — wellformedness clean-room (Unit C)

Scope: close the two gaps that kept `check_theory` from fully replacing
post-elaboration wellformedness checking. All behavior re-derived by probing
`oracle/wf_oracle.sh`; no tamarin-rs / Haskell source read. Round-4 probes are in
`scratchpad/probes4`, logged in `QUERIES.log`, spec'd in `BEHAVIOR.md`.

## GAP 1 — sort-aware quantifier binding

The wrong-form-terms check bound quantifiers by NAME only (round-2), which
over-binds. The reference distinguishes variable SORTS: a temporal binder `#x`
does not bind a message use `x` (`All #x. (A(x) @ #x) ==> (#x = #x)` ->
`Formula terms: `Free x'`), and a message binder `x` does not bind a node use
`#x` (-> `Free #x`). Fix: the de Bruijn binder stack now carries `(name, sort
class)` and matches on both. Sort classes collapse `{Untagged, Msg}` (which is
what the round-2 false positive actually needed) but keep Pub/Fresh/Node/Nat
distinct. De Bruijn indexing still counts every quantified var, so
`All x #x. ... h(x)` -> `h(Bound 1)` (probe g1_dbsort).

Probing the sort-annotation interaction surfaced a previously-unimplemented
topic, `Quantifier sorts`: quantifying over a public or fresh variable is
rejected (`Lemma `L' uses quantifiers with wrong sort: ("x",LSortPub)`); msg,
temporal and nat quantifiers are fine. It applies to lemmas and restrictions,
lists offending vars as Haskell `(name,LSort)` tuples in binding order with the
same width-69 fillSep wrap as Formula terms, and is emitted before Formula terms
within an item.

## GAP 2 — semantic guardedness

Replaced the round-3 heuristic (which recursed the guard through Or/Not/
quantifiers) with a decision procedure over the guarded fragment. Guard set of a
(sub)formula = the `(name, class)` keys of variables in ACTION atoms reachable
through conjunctions only; disjunction, negation, implication, `=`/`<`/`Mset`/
subterm/`last` atoms and nested quantifiers contribute no guards (probes
gx_disj_ant, gx_neg_ant, gx_less, gx_last, gx_quant_g, gx_eq_ant). A universal is
guarded only as `guard ==> rest`; any other body is "universal quantifier without
toplevel implication". An existential needs its variables covered by conjunctive
action guards, else "unguarded variable(s)". The first failing quantifier is
reported (antecedent before consequent, left before right — gr_both, gr_sib);
restrictions are lemma-only here (an unguarded restriction is a fatal error).

## Assembly and printer

- Per-item bundle. Quantifier sorts / Formula terms / Formula guardedness are
  emitted item-by-item (all lemmas in source order, then all restrictions), in
  that sub-order per item; consecutive same-topic entries merge under one header,
  non-consecutive ones become separate blocks (ord_qs_ft_qs -> QS,FT,QS as three
  blocks). `Lemma annotations` stays a separate global check after the bundle.
  New entry point `checks::formula_reports`.

- Col-relative formula printer. Round-3's `base+hang` was off by one at the top
  level (gt_and_two_all). The layout engine now hangs at `col + hang` (quantifier
  body `col+1`, binary/relational operands `col+0`), and relational atoms break
  at their operator with unparenthesised operands (gnest). Narrow formulas are
  byte-identical to the single-line printer, so all round-1..3 fixtures are
  unchanged.

## Tests

73 tests pass (was 53). 20 new byte-parity fixtures captured live from the oracle
(`tests/fixtures/g4_*.txt`) with hand-built ASTs: sort-aware binding (g1_core /
g1_msgnode / g1_dbsort), Quantifier sorts (pub, multi-var wrap, two lemmas,
restriction), per-item ordering (ord_qs_ft_qs, ord_la_ft), guardedness decision
(universal/existential disjunction, disjunction antecedent, ordering-atom
non-guard, partial coverage, recursion order, existential implication, mixed
conjunction), and the printer (top-level conjunction break, sibling nesting,
relational break + nested hang).

## Residual (documented in BEHAVIOR.md)

Formula-printer capture-avoidance (`$x.1` when a bound name collides with a free
name) is not reproduced; fixtures avoid collisions. Quantifier-head wrapping and
fact-argument wrapping are unprobed. Predicate atoms are expanded by the oracle
before the check, so they are not expected at the checker; guard extraction
treats them as non-guarding defensively.

content.content:
theory counter begin

builtins: multiset, symmetric-encryption, hashing

rule Create:
  [ Fr(~s) ]
  --[ Start(~s) ]->
  [ Counter(~s, '1')]

rule Inc:
  [ Counter( ~s, x ), In( x ) ] // The In(x) could be nicely prevented by ensuring
  --[ Counter( ~s, x ) ]->      // that x never contains names by using the sort
  [ Counter( ~s, x + '1' )      // system.
  , Out( senc( x,  ~s ) ) ]


/* How can I ensure this lemma directly? */
lemma counters_linear_order[reuse,use_induction]:
  "All x y #i #j s.
      Counter(s,x) @ i & Counter(s,y) @ j
      ==> (Ex z. x + z = y) | (Ex z. y + z = x) | y = x"

lemma counter_start[reuse,use_induction]:
  "All #i x s.
      Counter(s,x) @ i  ==> Ex #j. Start(s) @ j & #j < #i"

lemma counter_increases[reuse,use_induction]:
  "All x y #i #j s.
      Counter(s,x) @ i & Counter(s,y) @ j ==> #i < #j
      ==> Ex z. x + z = y"

lemma lesser_senc_secret[use_induction]:
  "All x y #i #j s.
      Counter(s,x) @ i & K(senc(h(y),s)) @ j
      ==> (#i < #j | Ex z. y + z = x)"

end

content.content:
theory issue515
begin

/** An example for issue 515 (https://github.com/tamarin-prover/tamarin-prover/issues/515)
*   In this example:
*    - rule "test" uses reserved names K, KU and KD, which should be reported.
*    - rule "test2" uses disallowed facts: Out on the left-hand-side and In on the right-hand-side
*    - rule "test3" uses reserved names K and In (in the middle)
*    - rule "test4" and rule "test5" use reserved names though they don't have the same capitalization 
*      with the reserved names.
* */

rule test:
[ K(x) ] --[ KU(x) ]-> [ KD(x) ]

rule test2:
[ Out(x)] --[ KD(x) ]-> [ In(x) ]

rule test3:
[ K(x) ] --[In(x) ]-> [ K(x) ]

rule test4:
[ Ku(x) ] --[ Ku(x) ]-> [ Out(x) ]

rule test5:
[ Kd(x) ] --[ Kd(x) ]-> [ Ku(x) ]

end---AXIOMS---
theory Axioms_and_Induction begin

/*
  Author: Simon Meier <simon.meier@inf.ethz.ch>
  Date:   2012 08 06
  Status: Working (exhibits soundness bug in tamarin-0.8.0.0)

  An example demonstrating that restrictions must be transformed as well when using
  induction.

*/

rule Start: [ Fr(x) ] --[ Start(x) ]-> [ A(x) ]

rule Step:  [ A(x) ]  --[ Step(x)  ]-> [ B(x) ]

rule Stop:  [ B(x) ]  --[ Stop(x)  ]-> [ ]

// Note that the set of traces satisfying this trace is *not* prefix-closed.
restriction Start_implies_Stop:
  "All x #i. Start(x) @ i ==> Ex #j. Stop(x) @ j"

lemma Exists_test [reuse]:
  exists-trace
  "All x #i. Step(x) @ i ==> F"

// Thus we cannot just add the restriction to the proof obligation resulting after
// the application of induction. We have to add the restriction before the
// transformation. This results in a wrong proof of the lemma.
lemma NoStep_with_induction [use_induction]:
  "All x #i. Step(x) @ i ==> F"

// Actually the property does not hold. Tamarin easily proves this when not
// using induction.
lemma NoStep_without_induction :
  "All x #i. Step(x) @ i ==> F"

/*
// When inlining the above restriction, induction would work again.
// Comment out the above restriction before proving the following lemma.
lemma NoStep_local_restriction_with_induction [use_induction]:
  "(All x #i. Start(x) @ i ==> Ex #j. Stop(x) @ j)
  ==>
   (All x #i. Step(x) @ i ==> F)"
*/

end

theory Cronto_EA 
begin

/* 
============
INTRODUCTION
============
* Protocol:	Cronto
* Modeler: 	Lara Schmid
* Date: 	March 2020
* Source:	[1] "Advancing the Formal Foundations for Voting Protocols", Lara Schmid, PhD Thesis, ETH Zürich.

=========
RESULTS: 
=========

// for -D=infallible
- lemma functional (exists-trace): verified (7 steps)
- lemma entity_authentication (all-traces): verified (60 steps)
- lemma device_authentication (all-traces): verified (60 steps)


// for -D=untrained
- lemma functional (exists-trace): verified (7 steps)
- lemma entity_authentication (all-traces): verified (17 steps)
- lemma device_authentication (all-traces): verified (17 steps)


*/


/* built-ins */
builtins: signing, asymmetric-encryption, hashing, symmetric-encryption
functions: otpw/1, pw/1 //otpw modeling MAC that is computed to do one time password
	// pw/1 is used to give password a type.

/* only allow human agent rules (corresponding to infallible human) */
#ifdef infallible
axiom only_infallible_humans:  
  	"not (Ex #k. H('Human') @k)" 
#endif

/* only allow for untrained human rules (corresponding to fallible human) */
#ifdef ruleBased
axiom only_fallible_humansRB:  
  	"not (Ex H x #k. H_role(H,x) @k)" 
#endif
#ifdef untrained
axiom only_fallible_humansU:  
  	"not (Ex H x #k. H_role(H,x) @k)"
#endif


/* untrained human rules*/ 
// insecure channels
rule H_fresh:
        [ Fr(~x) ]
	--[ Fresh($H,$x.1,~x), !HK($H,$x.1,~x),
		H($H) ]->
	[ !HK($H,$x.1,~x)]

rule H_send: 
	[ !HK($H,$x.1,x.2) ]
	--[ Send($H,$x.1,x.2),
		H($H) ]->
	[ Out(<$x.1,x.2>)]

rule H_receive: 
	[ In( <$x.1,x.2>) ]
	--[ Receive($H,$x.1,x.2), !HK($H,$x.1, x.2),
		H($H) ]->
	[ !HK($H,$x.1, x.2) ]

// secure channel
rule H_send_S:
  	[ !HK($H,$x.1,x.2) ]
	--[ Send($H,$x.1,x.2),
		H($H), To($A)]->
	[ Out_S($H,$A,$x.1,x.2) ]

theory Tgx_eq_ant
begin

predicates: Pr(x) <=> x = x
rule R1:
   [ Fr(~x) ]
  --[ A(~x), B(~x), C(~x) ]->
   [ Out(~x) ]
lemma L:
  all-traces
  "All x. (x = x) ==> (x = x)"
end
theory Tge_eqonly
begin

predicates: Pr(x) <=> x = x
rule R1:
   [ Fr(~x) ]
  --[ A(~x), B(~x), C(~x) ]->
   [ Out(~x) ]
lemma L:
  exists-trace
  "Ex x #i. (A(x) @ #i) & (x = x)"
end
theory Tge_disj
begin

predicates: Pr(x) <=> x = x
rule R1:
   [ Fr(~x) ]
  --[ A(~x), B(~x), C(~x) ]->
   [ Out(~x) ]
lemma L:
  exists-trace
  "Ex x #i. (A(x) @ #i) | (B(x) @ #i)"
end
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# gprobe.sh <tag> <trace: all|exists> <formula> [builtins]
# Builds a theory with rule R1 (A,B,C,P actions) and one lemma, prints the
# wellformedness block. Fact facts available in rule: A,B,C over ~x.
tag="$1"; trace="$2"; formula="$3"; builtins="${4:-}"
d=/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/workspace/scratchpad/probes4
q="all-traces"; [ "$trace" = "exists" ] && q="exists-trace"
cat > "$d/$tag.spthy" <<EOF
theory T$tag
begin
$builtins
predicates: Pr(x) <=> x = x
rule R1:
   [ Fr(~x) ]
  --[ A(~x), B(~x), C(~x) ]->
   [ Out(~x) ]
lemma L:
  $q
  "$formula"
end
EOF
/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/workspace/scratchpad/wf.sh "$d/$tag.spthy"
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run oracle, print ONLY the wellformedness block (success line or WARNING /* */).
ORACLE=/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/oracle/wf_oracle.sh
out="$("$ORACLE" "$@" 2>&1)"
if printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -qF 'All wellformedness checks were successful.'; then
  echo '/* All wellformedness checks were successful. */'
  exit 0
fi
# Extract the /* ... */ block that contains the WARNING header.
printf '%s\n' "$out" | awk '
  /^\/\*$/ { buf=$0"\n"; inblk=1; haswarn=0; next }
  inblk==1 {
    buf=buf $0 "\n";
    if ($0 ~ /WARNING: the following wellformedness/) haswarn=1;
    if ($0 ~ /^\*\/$/) { if (haswarn) printf "%s", buf; inblk=0 }
    next
  }
'

lemma injectivity[reuse]:
    "All id n1 n2 n3 #i #j on1 on2 #k.
        //Injective facts that are linked by n2
        Injectivity(id,n1,n2) @ i & Injectivity(id,n2,n3) @ j
        //And some other injective fact with the same id with relation to j
        & Injectivity(id,on1,on2) @ k & #k < #j
    ==> //can't be in between (might be equal to #i, though)
        not(#i < #k)"

//////////////////////////////////////////
//  Trace properties
lemma detect_sound:
    "All ki kr #t1.
        Detect(<ki, kr>) @ t1
    ==> (Ex #t0. #t0 < #t1 & Compromise(ki) @ t0)
        | (Ex #t0. #t0 < #t1 & Compromise(kr) @ t0)"

//Protocol Property without compromise
lemma correct_dolevyao:
    "All pks nonces c #t1.
        Session('R',pks,nonces,c) @ t1 & not(Ex k #tc. Compromise(k) @ tc)
    ==> Ex #t0. #t0 < #t1 & 
            Session('I',pks,nonces,c) @ t0"

lemma unmatching_implies_detect_with_R_uncompromised[use_induction]:
    "All keys nonces1 nonces2 c1 c2 #t1 #t2 #t3.
        #t1 < #t3 &
        //Given an matching session
        Begin('R', keys, c2) @ t3
        & Session('I', keys, nonces2, c2) @ t2
        //Where detection did not occur
        & not(Detect(keys) @ t2)
        //Then for all sessions before that
        & Session('R', keys, nonces1, c1) @ t1
    ==> //Either that session was also matching
        (Ex #t0. Session('I', keys, nonces1, c1) @ t0)
        //Or I's key was compromised
        | (Ex i r #tc. (keys = <i,r>) & Compromise(i) @ tc)"

content.content:
# Assembly / ordering of formula-bundle {Quantifier sorts, Formula terms, Formula guardedness}
ord_ftqs/ord_qsft :: per-item emission (sub-order QS,FT,GD); topics NOT globally fixed - determined by which lemma emits first
ord_qs_ft_qs :: L1=QS,L2=FT,L3=QS -> THREE blocks (QS,FT,QS); same-topic merges ONLY when CONSECUTIVE in emission order
ord_unbound_qs :: rule Unbound then lemma QS -> formula bundle sits after rule-topics (unchanged global slot)
qs_restr/ord_rl_ft2 :: LEMMAS processed before RESTRICTIONS (restriction src-first still emits after lemma); FT merge = lemma entry then restriction entry
ord_la_ft2 :: Lemma annotations is NOT in the per-item bundle -> emitted as a separate global check AFTER FT (L2 FT before L1 LemmaAnnot)
# GAP 2: guardedness decision procedure
gu_bareatom/gu_conj/gu_disj/gu_neg/gu_iff :: universal body not a top-level implication -> "universal quantifier without toplevel implication"
gu_impl_ok/gu_true :: universal guard==>rest with all vars guarded -> SUCCESS
ge_conj_ok/ge_bareact/ge_eqonly :: existential guarded by conjunction-reachable action atom(s) -> SUCCESS
ge_disj/ge_impl/ge_neg :: existential body not conjunction-of-actions -> "unguarded variable(s) ..." (all quantified vars)
ge_unguard/gv_vacuous/ge_noact :: existential with some/all vars not covered by action guards -> those vars "unguarded variable(s)" (binding order)
gx_conj_ant :: conjunction antecedent guards all -> SUCCESS
gx_disj_ant/gx_neg_ant :: disjunction/negation in antecedent does NOT guard (all vars unguarded)
gx_pred_grd/gx_eq_ant/gx_less/gx_last :: predicate(expanded)/equality/Less/last atoms do NOT guard; ONLY Action atoms guard
gx_less_act :: A(x)@#i & (#i<#j) -> only x,#i guarded; #j unguarded (Less non-guard)
gx_quant_g :: (Ex #j. A(x)@#j) ==> ... -> quantifier in antecedent does NOT guard (no descent into quantifiers)
gx_pair :: A(<x,y>)@#i guards x AND y (all vars of the action atom)
gx_nest_ante/gx_nest_cons :: recursion into antecedent AND consequent finds nested failing quantifier; reported sub=inner quantifier, whole=entire lemma formula
gr_both :: both antecedent+consequent broken -> ANTECEDENT reported first (recurse guard before rest)
gr_sib :: two broken siblings in a conjunction -> FIRST (left) reported
gr_restr :: unguarded RESTRICTION -> FATAL error (Guarded.hs:526), not a warning -> guardedness is LEMMA-ONLY
# Multi-line formula printer calibration (col-relative)
gt_and_two_all :: top-level A ∧ B breaks -> right operand hangs at formula start col (col+0), NOT base -> round-3 base+hang was off-by-one at top level
gnest :: nested quantifier body hang = start_col + 1 (measured All at col 9 -> body col 10); binop/relational right operand hang = start_col + 0
gnest :: relational atoms (=,<,...) BREAK at their operator (unparenthesised operands), like binops hang 0
gact :: action atom `fact @ temporal` rendered single-line (no internal break at observed widths); gneg: ¬ then parens(operand)
g1_samename :: Ex x #x. A(x)@#x -> SUCCESS (msg x and node #x are distinct binders, both bound)
g1_dbsort :: All x #x. ... x = h(x) -> h(Bound 1): msg x sits under node #x on the binder stack (all binders counted; match is sort-aware)
g1_msgnode :: All x #i. ... #x=#i -> Free #x (no node binder named x); msg binder x does not bind node use #x

input.command:
cat >> /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/workspace/QUERIES.log <<'EOF'
# ---- ROUND 5 probes (scratchpad/probes5/, via wf.sh -> wf_oracle.sh) ----
# Guardedness: equality-atom guards (existential)
g5_e_eqbare/g5_e_eqbarer :: Ex z. z='c' / 'c'=z -> SUCCESS (equality with one clean side guards the other side's vars)
g5_e_eqinner/g5_e_eqinner_r :: Ex z. h(z)='c' / 'c'=h(z) -> SUCCESS (guarded side vars may sit DEEP in the term)
g5_e_eqself/g5_e_eqself2 :: Ex z. z=z / z=h(z) -> unguarded 'z' (both sides contain the current var)
g5_e_eqtwo :: Ex y z. y=z -> unguarded 'y','z' (neither side clean)
g5_e_eqboth :: Ex y z. <y,z>=y -> unguarded 'y','z'
g5_e_eqpair :: All k #i. A(k)@#i ==> Ex y r. k=<y,r> -> SUCCESS (outer-bound side clean -> guards y,r)
g5_e_eqmset :: ... ==> Ex z. x+z=y (multiset) -> SUCCESS
g5_e_eqchain :: Ex z w. (z='c') & (w=h(z)) -> SUCCESS (left-to-right: z resolved, then h(z) clean)
g5_e_revchain :: Ex z w. (w=h(z)) & (z='c') -> unguarded 'w' -> SINGLE left-to-right pass over equalities, NO fixpoint
g5_e_cyc :: Ex z w. (z=h(w)) & (w=h(z)) -> unguarded 'z','w'
g5_e_actorder :: ... Ex y w #i. (w=h(y)) & (A(y)@#i) -> SUCCESS -> ALL action atoms are collected BEFORE the equality pass
g5_e_vac :: ... Ex y z. y='c' -> unguarded 'z' only (y eq-guarded; vacuous var unguarded)
g5_e_pairboth :: Ex y z. <y,z>='c' -> SUCCESS (clean side resolves ALL other-side vars at once)
g5_e_unif :: Ex z w. <z,'c'>=<'c',w> -> unguarded 'z','w' -> side-based, NOT unification
g5_e_selfnoop :: Ex z. (z='c') & (z=z) -> SUCCESS (equality over already-resolved vars is a no-op)
g5_e_disjeq :: Ex z. (z='c')|(z='d') -> unguarded 'z' (equality guards are conjunction-reachable only)
g5_e_neg_eq :: Ex z. not(z='c') -> unguarded 'z'
g5_e_eqfree :: Ex z. z=h(w) (w free) -> guardedness SILENT (free vars are "clean"); only Formula terms fires
g5_e_eqtemp :: ... ==> Ex #i. #i=#j -> SUCCESS (temporal equality guards the same way)
g5_e_lessonly :: ... ==> Ex #j. #j<#i -> unguarded '#j' (Less does NOT guard)
g5_e_lastonly :: Ex #i. last(#i) -> unguarded '#i' (last does NOT guard)
g5_e_subterm :: ... ==> Ex z. z<<x -> unguarded 'z' (subterm atom does NOT guard)
g5_e_actdeep :: Ex z #i. A(h(z))@#i -> SUCCESS (action atom guards vars DEEP in its args)
g5_e_conjnest :: Ex x. (Ex #i. A(x)@#i)&(Ex #j. C(x)@#j) -> unguarded 'x' (nested quantifiers guard NOTHING)
# Guardedness: universal antecedent equality guards
g5_a_eqg1/g5_a_eqg2 :: All w x #i. (A(x)@#i & w=h(x)) ==> C(w)@#i (either conjunct order) -> SUCCESS
g5_a_eqonly :: All w. (w='c') ==> (w='c') -> SUCCESS (pure equality guard works for universals)
g5_a_conseq_eq :: All w x #i. A(x)@#i ==> (w=h(x) & C(w)@#i) -> unguarded 'w' (guard region = antecedent only)
g5_a_disj_ant :: (A|C) ==> ... -> unguarded (disjunction antecedent still guards nothing; round-4 confirmed)
g5_a_iff :: All x #i. A<=>C -> "universal quantifier without toplevel implication" (unchanged)
# Guardedness: quantifier fusion
g5_e_nest :: Ex x. Ex #i. A(x)@#i -> SUCCESS (directly-nested same-kind quantifiers FUSE before the check)
g5_a_nest :: All x. All #i. A@#i ==> C@#i -> SUCCESS (forall fusion)
g5_a_nest_noimpl :: All x. All #i. A@#i -> "without toplevel implication"; report renders the FUSED form "∀ x #i. A( x ) @ #i"
g5_a_fuse_ex :: All x. Ex #i. A(x)@#i -> "without toplevel implication" (NO cross-kind fusion)
g5_e_fuse_all :: Ex x. All #i. (A==>C) -> unguarded 'x' (forall body guards nothing for the outer Ex)
# Variable identity includes the numeric index
g5_idx :: Ex y #i. A(y.1)@#i -> Formula terms `Free y.1' AND unguarded binder (renamed 'y.2' by capture-avoidance) -> binder identity = (name, INDEX, sort-class)
# Family 2: mismatching sorts/capitalization grouping
s5_idx :: rule with $x.1 and x.2 -> SILENT (grouping key includes the index)
s5_idxsame :: $x.1 and x.1 -> reported "1. $x.1, x.1" (index shown in the variant listing)
s5_capidx :: $X.1 and $x.1 -> reported "1. $X.1, $x.1" (same index, capitalization clash)
s5_capdiff :: $X.1 and $x.2 -> SILENT
# Family 3: reserved-name capitalization normalization
t5_ku_lhs :: [ Ku(x) ] premise -> Reserved names "on left-hand-side: !KU( x )"; NO lhs-not-rhs entry (normalized to builtin KU)
t5_ku_all :: Ku in all three positions -> three entries, all rendered !KU( x )
t5_kd_mid :: Kd action -> "on the middle: !KD( x )"
t5_up_inout :: [ IN(x) ] --[ OUT(x) ]-> [ FR(x) ] -> premise silent (IN=In legal), action "Out( x )" reserved-middle, conclusion "Fr( x )" special-facts -> In/Out/Fr/KU/KD match case-insensitively and render canonically
EOF
echo logged

input.command:
cd /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/workspace && python3 - <<'EOF'
import os, re, glob
src='scratchpad/corpus5_out'
dst='wf-clean/tests/corpus5'
os.makedirs(dst, exist_ok=True)
for f in sorted(glob.glob(src+'/*.out')):
    base=os.path.basename(f)[:-4]
    lines=open(f, encoding='utf-8').read().split('\n')
    i=0; n=0
    while i < len(lines):
        m=re.match(r'^(lemma|restriction)\s+([A-Za-z0-9_.-]+)', lines[i])
        if not m: i+=1; continue
        kind, name = m.group(1), m.group(2)
        # find first quote after this line
        j=i; buf=None; done=False
        while j < len(lines) and not done:
            line=lines[j]
            if buf is None:
                q=line.find('"')
                if q>=0:
                    rest=line[q+1:]
                    if rest.endswith('"'): buf=[rest[:-1]]; done=True
                    else: buf=[rest]
                elif j>i+3: break  # no formula nearby (e.g. diff lemma)
            else:
                s=line.strip()
                if s.endswith('"'): buf.append(s[:-1]); done=True
                else: buf.append(s)
            j+=1
        if done and buf is not None:
            formula=' '.join(x for x in (p.strip() if k>0 else p for k,p in enumerate(buf)) if x!='' or True)
            formula=' '.join(formula.split())
            out=os.path.join(dst, f'{base}__{kind}__{name}.fml')
            with open(out,'w',encoding='utf-8') as fh: fh.write(formula+'\n')
            n+=1
        i=j if done else i+1
print('wrote', len(glob.glob(dst+'/*.fml')), 'formulas')
EOF

import os, re, glob
src='scratchpad/corpus5_out'
dst='wf-clean/tests/corpus5'
os.makedirs(dst, exist_ok=True)
for f in sorted(glob.glob(src+'/*.out')):
    base=os.path.basename(f)[:-4]
    lines=open(f, encoding='utf-8').read().split('\n')
    i=0
    while i < len(lines):
        m=re.match(r'^(lemma|restriction)\s+([A-Za-z0-9_.-]+)', lines[i])
        if not m: i+=1; continue
        kind, name = m.group(1), m.group(2)
        # advance to the header terminator: a line ending with ':'
        j=i
        while j < len(lines) and not lines[j].rstrip().endswith(':'):
            j+=1
            if j > i+5: break
        if j >= len(lines) or not lines[j].rstrip().endswith(':'):
            i+=1; continue
        j+=1
        buf=None; done=False
        while j < len(lines) and not done:
            line=lines[j]
            if buf is None:
                q=line.find('"')
                if q>=0:
                    rest=line[q+1:]
                    if rest.endswith('"'): buf=[rest[:-1]]; done=True
                    else: buf=[rest]
                elif j>i+8: break
            else:
                s=line.strip()
                if s.endswith('"'): buf.append(s[:-1]); done=True
                else: buf.append(s)
            j+=1
        if done and buf is not None:
            formula=' '.join(p.strip() if k>0 else p for k,p in enumerate(buf))
            formula=' '.join(formula.split())
            with open(os.path.join(dst, f'{base}__{kind}__{name}.fml'),'w',encoding='utf-8') as fh:
                fh.write(formula+'\n')
        i=j if done else i+1
print('wrote', len(glob.glob(dst+'/*.fml')), 'formulas')
EOF

### Variable with mismatching sorts or capitalization  (round5: index-aware)
Trigger: within one rule, two variables share a base name AND numeric index but
differ in sort or capitalization. The grouping key is (lowercased name, index):
`$x.1` vs `x.2` is SILENT, `$x.1` vs `x.1` reports "1. $x.1, x.1" (probes
s5_idx/s5_idxsame/s5_capdiff). Suffix-sort spellings are the same variable as
their sigil form (`x:pub` = `$x`, silent - s5_suffix) and render with the
sigil (`x:pub` vs `~x` -> "$x, ~x" - s5_suffix2). Fixed header:
    "Possible reasons:\n1. Identifiers are case sensitive, i.e.,'x' and 'X' are considered to be different.\n2. The same holds for sorts:, i.e., '$x', 'x', and '~x' are considered to be different.\n"
per rule: "  rule `A5': \n    1. $s, ~s" (trailing space; numbered variant
groups). Variants are ordered by sort class $ < ~ < msg < % (s5_all4; Node
unobserved, placed last) and by exact name within a class ("$Ab, $aB" -
s5_capord; sort rank beats name order - s5_crossname "$x, ~X"). Groups are
sorted by their key and separated by a 4-space line (s5_groups). Rule entries
join with "\n  \n" (issue527 target).

### Reserved names  (round5: builtin normalization)
Trigger: rule uses a fact with a reserved name in a position. The reserved set
is POSITION-DEPENDENT (observed z9/z11/z12):
  - left-hand-side / right-hand-side: {K, KU, KD}
  - the middle (actions): {K, KU, KD, In, Out, Fr}  (the I/O facts are also

BUILTIN NORMALIZATION (round5, t5_ku_lhs/t5_ku_all/t5_kd_mid/t5_up_inout +
issue515 target): fact names matching KU/KD/In/Out/Fr CASE-INSENSITIVELY
denote the builtin facts and render canonically - `Ku(x)` reports `!KU( x )`
(persistent), `Kd` -> `!KD( x )`, `OUT`/`FR` -> `Out( x )`/`Fr( x )`; an `IN`
premise is a legal In premise (silent). `K` is single-letter (only the exact
spelling parses). Normalized builtins never produce Fact-capitalization
conflicts (issue515 has Ku/KU/Kd/KD and no such topic).
Per (rule, position) entry joined "\n  \n":
    "  Rule `R1' contains facts with reserved names on left-hand-side:\n    <facts>"
Position phrases: LHS `on left-hand-side:`, actions `on the middle:`,
